mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cut periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing equity valuations to contract over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength that boosts corporate revenue expectations, driving sustained positive equity returns over a quarter-long horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, with 60-day forward returns showing negative alpha or Rank IC below 0.03 during periods of synchronized growth.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, which compresses near-term profit margins and triggers short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of industrial production contraction episodes, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 2.5 percent annualized threshold forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations within one month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPIAUCSL exceeding 2.5 percent, yielding positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered cases, or Rank IC turns negative over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 45 percent of observations, or interaction effects vanish when tested against additive single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which correlates with broader economic momentum and supports positive equity market performance over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts show no correlation with 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all market cycles, or positive housing data coincides with equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production following a GDP contraction indicates successful supply-side recovery, reducing input cost pressures and restoring corporate profitability, which drives short-term equity rallies.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 55 percent of post-recovery supply rebounds, or the signal fails to generate positive alpha during documented recovery periods.
